summary: Dual auth strategy fix verified. All tests pass: Login returns tokens in JSON body, /api/auth/me works with Bearer header (no cookies), /api/auth/refresh accepts token in body, all protected endpoints work with Authorization header. Frontend saves tokens to localStorage, dashboard loads without 'Session expirée', subscription plan changes work, navigation works, logout clears tokens, driver login works.

critical_code_review_comments: tokenStore.js: Correctly saves/retrieves/clears tokens from localStorage using tp_access_token and tp_refresh_token keys
AuthContext.jsx: Correctly saves tokens on login/register, sends via header, clears on logout
api.js: Request interceptor correctly attaches Bearer token from localStorage, 401 interceptor sends refresh_token in body
server.py: Login/register return tokens in body, refresh accepts token from cookie/body/header (belt and suspenders approach)
server.py: get_current_user checks cookie first, then Authorization header - dual auth strategy working
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_DualAuth_Recipient delivery during backend testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dual auth strategy verified in iteration 10. The fix for 'Session expirée' / 'No refresh token' error on mobile is working. Key implementation: 1) Login/register return access_token and refresh_token in JSON body. 2) Frontend saves tokens to localStorage (tp_access_token, tp_refresh_token). 3) axios request interceptor attaches Bearer token from localStorage. 4) 401 interceptor sends refresh_token in body for refresh. 5) Backend accepts token from cookie, body, or Authorization header. Firestore permission errors in console are non-blocking and expected in preview environment.

mocked_apis.firestore_sync: May fail with Permission Denied in preview environment (non-blocking)

session_expired_fix.original_issue: 'Session expirée' / 'No refresh token' error on mobile due to cookies being unreliable (SameSite=none, Secure blocked by anti-tracking)
session_expired_fix.fix_applied: Dual auth strategy - cookies (primary) + localStorage tokens (fallback). Backend returns tokens in response body, accepts refresh_token in request body, accepts Authorization header.
session_expired_fix.verification_status: FIXED - All auth flows work with Bearer token (no cookies needed), tokens persist in localStorage across page refreshes
